customer_anxiety_profile: Chris is the functional decision-maker and bill-payer; Janice initiated the inquiry. Chris is analytically oriented — he came to the call with specific technical concerns about roof orientation and tree shading, not general skepticism. His cooperation increased once the credit qualifier was introduced, but the shading concern was not resolved, only bypassed. No hostility; moderate caution on the technical fit question.
unresolved_concerns: Roof orientation: Chris stated the eave runs north-south, which he described as 'not real conducive to black panels' — this concern was not addressed with any data, only reassured away.
Seasonal shading: Chris stated they don't see sun until noon in winter due to a row of tall fir trees on the east side — same dismissal, no resolution.

Janice's credit: Janice herself expressed some uncertainty about her own score ('It won't work for me'), though Chris indicated Janice's credit is the qualifying one ('that'll work for her'). The closer should confirm Janice's credit is the account holder and that her score meets the 670 threshold.
sensitive_topics_raised: Janice's brother is on the title as a beneficiary (estate planning arrangement, not a co-decision-maker — no appointment inclusion needed).
Janice's credit is the qualifying credit per Chris's confirmation; Janice herself expressed some uncertainty about her own score, but Chris indicated it should work.
exact_promises_rep_made: Appointment booked for tomorrow at 10AM as a phone call.

closer_should_know: Chris raised a two-part shading concern (north-south roof orientation + fir trees blocking morning sun in winter) that Meg dismissed with 'I'm not too worried about it' — no data, no routing. The closer must address this directly and early, either validating the concern with the actual design data or reframing it. Walking past it will confirm Chris's doubt; contradicting Meg's reassurance without acknowledgment will create a credibility gap. Lead with the design showing the actual sun exposure numbers before Chris raises it again.
